newsSince: Positive operational momentum. FQ2 2026 came in strong (~$2.28B revenue) with a raised FY26 outlook on post-Ansys software scale, and in June Synopsys launched its first Multiphysics Fusion solutions (Ansys signoff + AI design tools + NVIDIA GPU acceleration), with early wins; shares jumped ~5.4% on June 17. Still a large (~$90B), well-discovered EDA name.

sources.label: Synopsys jumps on raised FY26 outlook + Ansys momentum (QuiverQuant)
Multiphysics Fusion / Ansys integration (Simply Wall St)

verdicts.rankRationale: On the crowding-sensitive CPO lens, an ~$90B fully-discovered EDA duopolist gains no hidden-bottleneck status from the Multiphysics Fusion launch. The beat-and-raise plus a still-negative YTD modestly improve entry asymmetry, worth a small positive drift but not a rank move.
verdicts.bull: Photonics/chiplet verification + Ansys multiphysics deepen design-flow moat.
verdicts.neutral: Quality compounder, but large and well-owned; limited residual CPO alpha.
verdicts.bear: Integration/regulatory and valuation overhang on a discovered name.
verdicts.primaryRisk: EDA spending normalization or integration friction

verdicts.rankRationale: On the under-owned-bottleneck master list, the raised FY26 outlook and June Multiphysics Fusion launch reinforce design-validation exposure against a still-negative YTD reset. Page already moved SNPS up; no need to move again, but the improving setup against a beat-and-raise warrants a small positive drift.
verdicts.bull: EDA/IP duopoly plus AI-chip complexity tailwind and Ansys multiphysics upsell.
verdicts.neutral: Core bottleneck but heavily covered; modest residual alpha.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: FY26 guide raise / Ansys synergy capture
